rulings.Case Title: DANIEL TARBELL SR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Noto Compel The Deposition Of William H. Harvey Co'S Person(S) Most Qualified And Custodian(S) Of Records Or Other Qualified Persons And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. PLAINTIFF DANIEL TARBELL'S MOTION to Compel The Deposition Of William H. Harvey Co's Person(s) Most Qualified And Custodian(s) Of Records or Other Qualified Persons And To Produce Documents At Deposition is off calendar per request of moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT RUHL et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Deft B.R. Funsten & Company And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs ROBERT RUHL, BEVERLY RUHL¿s motion to compel and request for sanctions is continued to 3/24/10 to give the parties a further opportunity to meet and confer over the scope of the depositions of Defendant BR Funsten¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records. If all issues raised by the motion have not been resolved before then, by 4pm on March 23 the parties, without any argument, must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org specifying what issue or issues remain in dispute. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PERKINS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Oltmans Construction Company¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Production Of Decedent Charles Perkins¿ Unredacted Medical Billing Records
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Oltmans Construction¿s motion to compel production of decedent Charles Perkins¿ unredacted medical billing records is granted. The information sought is relevant or likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the issue of plaintiff¿s damages under Nishihama v. City and County of San Francisco (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 298 and Hanif v. Housing Authority (1988) 200 Cal.App.3d 635. = (220/HEK)
